revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Shinto shrine]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/24755395338|time triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
[[commons:Category:Ikenimasu-asagirikihatahime-jinja]][[ja:池坐朝霧黄幡比売神社]] {{Infobox religious building | name = Ikenimasu-asagirikihatahime Shrine | native_name = 池坐朝霧黄幡比売神社 | image = Ikenimasu Jinja Haiden.jpg | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 15 | mapframe-wikidata = yes | mapframe-point = none | map_type = Japan | map_caption = Ikenimasu-asagirikihatahime Shrine | coordinates = {{coord|34.566049|135.808527}} | map_relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | deity = {{ill|Takuhadachiji-hime|lt=Takuhadachiji-hime no Mikoto|de|Yorozuhatahime|en|Takuhadachiji-hime|ja|栲幡千千姫命|ru|Ёродзухататоёакицуси-химэ-но микото|zh|栲幡千千姬命|qid=Q11110848|1=Takuhadachiji-hime}} }} {{nihongo|'''Ikenimasu-asagirikihatahime Shrine'''|池坐朝霧黄幡比売神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Yamato Province]]. == instance of (P31) == * {{ill|Shinto shrine|lt=Shinto shrine|ar|جينجا|arz|ضريح شنتو|bcl|Shinto shrine|ca|Jinja|cs|Šintoistická svatyně|da|Shinto-tempel|de|Shintō-Schrein|el|Σιντοϊστικό Ιερό|en|Shinto shrine|eo|Ŝintoa sanktejo|es|Jinja|eu|Jinja|fa|معبد شینتویی|fi|Šintolainen pyhäkkö|fr|Sanctuaire shinto|hi|शिंतो मंदिर|hr|Šintoistički hram|hu|Sintó szentély|id|Jinja|it|Jinja|ja|神社|ka|ძინძია|ko|신사 (신토)|lb|Shintō-Schräin|mr|शिंतो मंदिर|nl|Jinja (heiligdom)|no|Jinja|or|ଜିଞ୍ଜା|pl|Jinja|pt|Templo xintoísta|ru|Дзиндзя|sc|Jinja|simple|Shinto shrine|sl|Šintoistično svetišče|sr|Шинтоистички храм|sv|Jinja|th|ศาลเจ้าชินโต|tr|Şinto tapınağı|uk|Святилище (синто)|vi|Thần xã|wuu|神社|zh_yue|神社|zh|神社|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} * {{ill|Shikinai Taisha|lt=Shikinai Taisha|en|Shikinai Taisha|ja|式内大社|simple|Shikinai Taisha|qid=Q134917288|1=Shikinai Taisha}} * {{ill|Q134917286|ltq=Q134917286|qid=Q134917286|1=Shikinaisha}} * {{ill|Q135009157|ltq=Q135009157|qid=Q135009157|1=Shrines receiving Tsukinami-sai and Niiname-sai and Ainame-sai offerings}} * {{ill|Kanpei-sha|lt=Kanpei-sha|simple|Kanpei-sha (Engishiki Jinmyocho)|qid=Q135160338|1=Kanpei-sha}} * {{ill|Shikinai Hiteisha|lt=Shikinai Hiteisha|ja|比定社|qid=Q135026601|1=Shikinai Hiteisha}} == part of (P361) == * {{ill|Q11433427|ltq=Q11433427|qid=Q11433427|1=List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province}} ** Series ordinal: 144 ** P155: {{ill|Q11523074|ltq=Q11523074|qid=Q11523074|1=Muraya-ni-masu-Mifutsuhime Shrine}} ** P156: {{ill|Kagamitsukuri Nimasu Amateru Mitama Shrine|lt=Kagamitsukuri Nimasu Amateru Mitama Shrine|ja|鏡作坐天照御魂神社|qid=Q38278496|1=Kagamitsukuri Nimasu Amateru Mitama Shrine}} == located in the administrative territorial entity (P131) == * {{ill|Q1203159|ltq=Q1203159|qid=Q1203159|1=Tawaramoto, Nara}} * {{ill|Q907495|ltq=Q907495|qid=Q907495|1=Yamato Province}} * {{ill|Shikige district|lt=Shikige district|ja|式下郡|zh|式下郡|qid=Q11486987|1=Shikige district}} == coordinate location (P625) == * {{coord|34.566049|135.808527}} == image (P18) == * Ikenimasu Jinja Haiden.jpg == dedicated to (P825) == * {{ill|Takuhadachiji-hime|lt=Takuhadachiji-hime no Mikoto|de|Yorozuhatahime|en|Takuhadachiji-hime|ja|栲幡千千姫命|ru|Ёродзухататоёакицуси-химэ-но микото|zh|栲幡千千姬命|qid=Q11110848|1=Takuhadachiji-hime}} == described by source (P1343) == * {{ill|Q135037878|ltq=Q135037878|qid=Q135037878|1=Kokugakuin University Shrine database (old)}} ** P2699: https://web.archive.org/web/20041122180323/http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp:80/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=22804 == Kokugakuin University Digital Museum entry ID (P13677) == * 180803 ** P3831: {{ill|Kokugakuin University Shrine database|lt=Kokugakuin University Shrine database|qid=Q135159299|1=Kokugakuin University Shrine database}} == modern shrine ranking (P13723) == * {{ill|Q134917316|ltq=Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} ** End time: {'time': '+1946-02-02T00:00:00Z', 'timezone': 0, 'before': 0, 'after': 0, 'precision': 11, 'calendarmodel': 'http://www.wikidata.org/entity/Q1985727'} ** P1534: {{ill|Q3029647|ltq=Q3029647|qid=Q3029647|1=Shinto Directive}} == P14005 (P14005) == * {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位|ko|종5위|zh|從五位|qid=Q11071125|1=Junior Fifth Rank}} == country (P17) == * ᥓᥣᥱ ᥙᥣᥢᥱ|tet|Japaun|te|జపాన్|tg|Ҷопон|th|ประเทศญี่ปุ่น|thnews|หมวดหมู่:ประเทศญี่ปุ่น|tig|ጃፓን|ti|ጃፓን|tk|Ýaponiýa|tl|Hapon|tly|Japonijə|tn|Japan|tok|ma Nijon|to|Siapani|tpi|Siapan|trv|Tanah Tunux|tr|Japonya|trnews|Kategori:Japonya|trquote|Japonya|trvoyage|Japonya|ts|Japani|tt|Япония|tum|Japan|tw|Japan|tyv|Япония|ty|Tāpōnē|udm|Япония|ug|ياپونىيە|uk|Японія|uknews|Категорія:Японія|ukquote|Японія|ukvoyage|Японія|ur|جاپان|uz|Yaponiya|vec|Japon|vep|Japonii|ve|Japan|vi|Nhật Bản|vibooks|Các quốc gia/Nhật Bản|vivoyage|Nhật Bản|vls|Japan|vo|Yapän|war|Hapon|wa|Djapon|wo|Sapoŋ|wuu|日本|xal|Ниxуудин Нутг|xh|IJapani|xmf|იაპონია|yi|יאפאן|yo|Japan|za|Nyiedbonj|zea|Japan|zgh|ⵏⵉⴱⵓⵏ|zh_classical|日本|zh_min_nan|Ji̍t-pún|zh_yue|日本|zh|日本|zhnews|Category:日本|zhquote|日本|zhvoyage|日本|zu|IJaphani|qid=Q17|1=Japan}} == name in kana (P1814) == * イケ- == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 4150005005224 == Commons category (P373) == * Ikenimasu-asagirikihatahime-jinja == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '奈良県磯城郡田原本町法貴寺502', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Ikenimasu-asagirikihatahime-jinja}} [[JA:池坐朝霧黄幡比売神社]] {{wikidata link|Q43594590}} == Japanese content == {{Shinto shrine |Name=Ikenimasu Asagiri Kihatahime Shrine |Image=[[Image:Ikenimasu Jinja Haiden.jpg|270px]]
Haiden (worship hall)
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} |Location={{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|qid=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Shiki District|Q1155036|qid=Q1155036|1=Shiki District, Nara}}{{ill|lt=Tawaramoto Town|Q1203159|qid=Q1203159|1=Tawaramoto, Nara}} 502 Hokiji | Latitude degrees = 34 | Latitude minutes = 33 | Latitude seconds = 57.8 | N/S = N | Longitude degrees = 135 | Longitude minutes = 48 | Longitude seconds = 30.7 | E/W = E | Map country code = JP |Enshrined deity={{ill|lt=Takuhata Chichihime no Mikoto|Q11110848|qid=Q11110848|lt=Ama no Yoro Takuhata Chihata Hime no Mikoto|1=Takuhadachiji-hime}}
{{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|qid=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}} |Shrine rank={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} (major)
Formerly {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |Main hall style={{ill|lt=Kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|qid=Q3193781|1=Kasuga-zukuri}} |Annual festival={{ill|lt=October 18|Q2964|qid=Q2964|1=October 18}} |Other name=Ike Shrine |Founded=(traditional) 24th year of {{ill|lt=Emperor Suiko|Q298057|qid=Q298057|1=Empress Suiko}} ({{ill|lt=616|Q33263|qid=Q33263|1=616}}) |Map=Japan Nara }} [[File:Ikenimasu Jinja Torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Ikenimasu Asagiri Kihatahime Shrine''' (いけにますあさぎりきはたひめじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|qid=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Shiki District|Q1155036|qid=Q1155036|1=Shiki District, Nara}}{{ill|lt=Tawaramoto Town|Q1203159|qid=Q1203159|1=Tawaramoto, Nara}}. It is a {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha (major shrine)|Q134917288|qid=Q134917288|1=Shikinai Taisha}}, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|qid=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. === History === === Foundation === The exact date of foundation is unknown, but according to a stone monument standing within the precincts, “In the 24th year of {{ill|lt=Emperor Suiko|Q298057|qid=Q298057|1=Empress Suiko}} ({{ill|lt=616|Q33263|qid=Q33263|1=616}}), the {{ill|lt=Hata clan|Q987290|qid=Q987290|1=Hata clan}}, who were granted the temple compound of Hōkiji founded by {{ill|lt=Prince Shōtoku|Q263972|qid=Q263972|1=Prince Shōtoku}}, revered this ancient shrine as the guardian deity of their ancestral god.” Based on this, it is inferred that the shrine was founded in the 24th year of Emperor Suiko by the Hata clan at the same time as the establishment of Hōkiji, and that it was venerated as the tutelary shrine of Hōkiji. === Overview === In the 2nd year of {{ill|lt=Tenpyō|Q1199879|qid=Q1199879|1=Tenpyō}} ({{ill|lt=730|Q23563|qid=Q23563|1=730}}), it appears as “Ike-no-Kami” in the *Ōyamato no Kuni Shōzeichō* ({{ill|lt=Shōsōin documents|Q11545362|qid=Q11545362|1=Shōsōin Documents}}). In the first year of {{ill|lt=Daidō (Japan)|Q1373191|qid=Q1373191|lt=Daidō|1=Daidō}} ({{ill|lt=806|Q30021|qid=Q30021|1=806}}), three households were donated to it as {{ill|lt=shinsen|Q1689534|qid=Q1689534|1=Shinpo}} (as recorded in the *{{ill|lt=Shinshō Kakuchōfu-shō|Q11502312|qid=Q11502312|1=Shinshō Kyakuchokufushō}}*). In the *{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}*, it is listed as a major shrine and is noted as receiving offerings at the monthly, Ainame, and Niiname festivals. In the 9th year of {{ill|lt=Tengyō|Q1327340|qid=Q1327340|1=Tengyō}} ({{ill|lt=949|Q24352|qid=Q24352|1=949}}), a {{ill|lt=Tenmangū|Q798838|qid=Q798838|1=Tenmangū}} was invited from {{ill|lt=Kitano Tenman-gū|Q662176|qid=Q662176|1=Kitano Tenmangū}} and enshrined together, and from the {{ill|lt=medieval period|Q12554|qid=Q12554|1=Middle Ages}} through the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|qid=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, it was known by the name “Tenman-gū.” According to the *{{ill|lt=Shiki District|Q1155036|qid=Q1155036|1=Shiki District, Nara}} Gazetteer*, “Hōkiji is an old temple said to have been founded by {{ill|lt=Hata no Kawakatsu|Q5680877|qid=Q5680877|1=Hata no Kawakatsu}}, and is the clan temple of the {{ill|lt=Hasegawa clan (Ariwara lineage)|Q11653880|qid=Q11653880|lt=Hasegawa faction|1=Hasegawa clan}}. Therefore, another name for the Hasegawa faction is the people of Hōkiji. This shrine was likely invited as the tutelary deity of that temple compound. From ancient times, the temple managed its rites, and the Hasegawa faction’s involvement in them likely originates from this.” From this, it is said that Hōkiji was revered as the clan temple of the Hasegawa faction, and this shrine was likewise worshipped. Furthermore, in the *Meishukushū* written by {{ill|lt=Konparu Zenpō|Q1780792|qid=Q1780792|1=Konparu Zenchiku}} of the {{ill|lt=Konparu school|Q4525036|qid=Q4525036|1=Konparu}} of {{ill|lt=Noh theatre|Q1037119|qid=Q1037119|1=Nōgaku}}, who was the son-in-law of {{ill|lt=Zeami|Q169352|qid=Q169352|1=Zeami Motokiyo}}, it is written: “Of the three sons of Hata no Kawakatsu, one transmitted martial arts, one transmitted music, and one transmitted sarugaku. The descendants who transmitted martial arts are the present Hasegawa faction of Yamato.” From this, it is thought that the shrine also had close ties with the Hata clan. In the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Meiji period|1=Meiji era|qid=Q3125096}}, this shrine, known as the Tenjin of Hōkiji Village, came to be regarded as Ikenimasu Asagiri Kihatahime Shrine, and in the 15th year of {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|qid=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} ({{ill|lt=1882|Q7812|qid=Q7812|1=1882}}), it was ranked as a {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. === Divine rank === * 3rd year of {{ill|lt=Ten’an (Japan)|Q1075611|qid=Q1075611|lt=Ten’an|1=Ten'an}} ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|qid=Q23897|1=859}}), {{ill|lt=January 27|Q2284|qid=Q2284|1=January 27}}: promoted from Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade to Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade (*{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|qid=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}*) — recorded as “Ikenimasu Asagiri Kihatahime no Kami” === Shrine precincts === Ikenimasu Jinja Honden.jpg|Main hall Ikenimasu Jinja Keidai.jpg|Shrine precincts Ikenimasu Jinja keidaisha1.jpg|Auxiliary shrine (left) Ikenimasu Jinja Keidaisha2.jpg|Auxiliary shrine (right) Houkiji Mon Toro.jpg|Hōkiji Senman-in temple gate and site of the bell within the precincts === Access === * From {{ill|lt=Kintetsu Railway|Q1531085|qid=Q1531085|1=Kintetsu Railway}} {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kashihara Line|1=UNKNOWN}} {{ill|lt=Iwami Station|Q6100492|qid=Q6100492|1=Iwami Station (Nara)}}, 20 minutes on foot east (local trains only); from {{ill|lt=Tawaramoto Station|Q7689224|qid=Q7689224|1=Tawaramoto Station}}, 30 minutes on foot northeast (local and express trains stop) * From {{ill|lt=West Japan Railway Company|Q502125|qid=Q502125|1=West Japan Railway Company}} (JR West) {{ill|lt=Sakurai Line|Q865801|qid=Q865801|1=Sakurai Line}} {{ill|lt=Yanagimoto Station|Q4188827|qid=Q4188827|1=Yanagimoto Station}}, 40 minutes on foot west === Related items === * {{ill|lt=Hata no Kawakatsu|Q5680877|qid=Q5680877|1=Hata no Kawakatsu}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Ikenimasu-asagirikihatahime-jinja}} * [http://www.town.tawaramoto.nara.jp/kanko/kanko/temple/3445.html Ike Shrine] – Tawaramoto Town * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=22804 Ikenimasu Asagiri Yokohata Hime Shrine] – Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program “Shinto and Shrine Historical Sources” {{Shinto stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Ikenimasu Asagiri Kihatahime Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ikenimasu Asagiri Kihatahime Shrine]][[Category:Shrines receiving Tsukinami-sai and Niiname-sai and Ainame-sai offerings]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:田原本町の建築物]] [[Category:Municipal History of Tawaramoto]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Nara Prefecture]]
